metadata.title: Tornado Cash & Samourai Wallet - privacy-tool prosecutions, developer liability, the code-is-speech axis (evidence-graded)
metadata.WARNING: EVIDENCE-GRADED. Three strands are kept STRICTLY SEPARATE and graded independently: (A) the FACT that DPRK/Lazarus and other criminals used these tools to launder funds; (B) the FACT that key legal theories used against them were contested or partly REJECTED by courts/agencies; (C) documented PROSECUTORIAL-CONDUCT problems. No defendant is described as more or less guilty than the court record states (convictions, pleas, deadlocks, appeals are stated precisely). This is the privacy-vs-surveillance axis - the repo's anti-digital-ID/anti-surveillance spine - NOT an endorsement of money laundering.
metadata.method: Primary where possible (DOJ/SDNY + Treasury/OFAC press releases, Fifth Circuit opinion, FBI, Dutch court coverage, CoinDesk/DL News/Mayer Brown/DeFi Education Fund).

facts: Tornado Cash was estimated to have laundered >$7.6B in virtual assets since 2019 (Treasury/coverage).

DPRK's Lazarus Group used Tornado Cash to launder >$455M from the Mar 2022 Ronin/Axie bridge hack (largest crypto heist at the time), >$96M from the Jun 2022 Harmony Horizon bridge, and >=$7.8M from the Aug 2022 Nomad heist.
The Dutch court found Pertsev's Tornado Cash laundered ~$2.2B in stolen/hacked crypto.
Samourai Wallet (Whirlpool CoinJoin + Ricochet) processed substantial volume DOJ characterized as including criminal proceeds.
strand_A_criminal_use_FACTS.honest_note: These harms are REAL. The privacy-tool critique below is NOT that laundering didn't happen - it's that the LEGAL MEANS used to prosecute were contested and the conduct sometimes improper.

facts: OFAC sanctioned Tornado Cash Aug 8 2022 (added smart-contract addresses to the SDN list) - the first time an autonomous, immutable smart-contract protocol was sanctioned.

Van Loon v. Department of the Treasury (5th Cir., Nov 26 2024): a unanimous panel held Tornado Cash's IMMUTABLE smart contracts are NOT 'property' under IEEPA ('just software code', no owner/counterparty), so OFAC 'overstepped its congressionally defined authority.'
OFAC DELISTED Tornado Cash on Mar 21 2025, removing the sanctions, citing review of 'novel legal and policy issues.'
FinCEN had told SDNY prosecutors (Aug 2023, ~6 months BEFORE charges) that, under its guidance, Samourai Wallet did NOT qualify as a money-transmitting business / did not need a license.
strand_B_legal_theories_REJECTED_or_CONT.what_it_does_not_show: The 5th Circuit ruling was about SANCTIONS authority over immutable code, not about whether individuals can be criminally liable - those are separate questions (and the criminal cases proceeded). Delisting was discretionary, not a finding that Tornado Cash was lawful.

strand_C_prosecutorial_conduct.grade: Samourai Brady/timing facts STRONG; characterization as bad-faith CONTESTED
facts: SDNY charged Samourai's Keonne Rodriguez (CEO) and William Lonergan Hill (CTO) in Apr 2024 with conspiracy to commit money laundering + conspiracy to run an unlicensed money-transmitting business.

Prosecutors did NOT disclose the Aug 2023 FinCEN communication (that Samourai wasn't a money transmitter) to the court/defense until Apr 1 2025 - a delayed disclosure the defense framed as a Brady violation and basis for dismissal.
Apr 7 2025: Deputy AG Todd Blanche issued 'Ending Regulation by Prosecution', directing DOJ to stop targeting non-custodial wallets/mixers for end-user conduct.

Despite the memo, SDNY proceeded: Rodriguez (Jul 29 2025) and Hill (Jul 30 2025) pled GUILTY to Count II (unlicensed money transmitting conspiracy); Count I (money-laundering conspiracy) was DISMISSED per the pleas. Rodriguez sentenced to 5 years, Hill to 4 years.

Tornado Cash: Roman Storm tried in SDNY; Aug 6 2025 MIXED verdict - convicted on conspiracy to operate an unlicensed money-transmitting business; jury DEADLOCKED on the money-laundering and sanctions-violation conspiracies. DOJ moved (Mar 9 2026) to RETRY the deadlocked counts (~Oct 2026).
strand_C_prosecutorial_conduct.what_it_does_not_show: Guilty pleas are convictions; the developers are not 'exonerated.' But the SEQUENCE - charge despite FinCEN guidance, suppress that guidance ~18 months, proceed even after a DOJ memo disfavoring such cases - is a documented process problem and the strongest 'selective/aggressive prosecution' example in the corpus (ties spec-sec-sdny-regulatory.json).

international_dimension.pertsev_netherlands: Alexey Pertsev (Russian national, Tornado Cash dev) convicted by a Dutch court (May 14 2024), sentenced to 64 months for laundering ~$2.2B; briefly released under electronic monitoring (Feb 2025) then continued detention pending appeal (Court of Appeal 's-Hertogenbosch). Shows the developer-liability question is being litigated in MULTIPLE jurisdictions, not just the US.
international_dimension.semenov: Co-founder Roman Semenov was also OFAC-designated (Aug 2023) and charged; remains at large (reportedly Russia).

edges.notes: First sanction of an immutable smart-contract protocol; delisted after the 5th Cir. loss.
Immutable smart contracts not 'property' under IEEPA; OFAC overstepped.
DPRK Lazarus: $455M Ronin + $96M Harmony + $7.8M Nomad; ~$7.6B total platform volume since 2019. The real-harm strand (A).
Guilty: unlicensed money-transmitting conspiracy; deadlocked on ML + sanctions; DOJ to retry (~Oct 2026).
Rodriguez 5y, Hill 4y on unlicensed-money-transmitting conspiracy; ML count dismissed per plea. FinCEN-guidance suppression (Brady) + proceeding post-Blanche-memo = strand C.
FinCEN told prosecutors Samourai was NOT a money transmitter ~6mo before charges; not disclosed until Apr 1 2025.
64 months for laundering ~$2.2B; multi-jurisdiction developer-liability question.

overall_verdict: The honest reading requires holding THREE true things at once: (A) Tornado Cash and Samourai were REALLY used by DPRK/Lazarus and others to launder large sums ($7.6B+ TC total; $455M Ronin etc.) - the harm is real; (B) the SANCTIONS theory against immutable code was REJECTED (Van Loon, 5th Cir.; OFAC delisting Mar 2025) and a key money-transmitter theory was CONTRADICTED by FinCEN's own guidance (Samourai); yet (C) developers were still convicted/pled (Pertsev 64mo NL; Storm guilty on 1 of 3, retrial pending; Rodriguez 5y/Hill 4y) - and in Samourai the government SUPPRESSED the exculpatory FinCEN communication for ~18 months and proceeded even after DOJ's own 'Ending Regulation by Prosecution' memo. This is the cleanest privacy-vs-surveillance case study in the corpus: the same state apparatus that pushes digital-ID/age-verification surveillance (digitalid-*, age-verification-abolition) simultaneously criminalizes financial-privacy tooling - on legal theories courts and agencies partly rejected - while real laundering (a genuine problem) supplies the justification. It ties directly to the SDNY/'regulation by prosecution' thread (spec-sec-sdny-regulatory) and the DPRK/Lazarus thread (Tasks #5/#7). Kept as an OVERLAY; not used in the formal proofs.
